events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: commercial construction, service entrance & metering, observed field condition: unsealed weatherhead penetration. Work actively in progress. Reference NEC Art. 230. Need identify safety hazards, severity, immediate controls. Decision fail because non-compliant/unsafe? Let's reason.

Unsealed weatherhead penetration: weatherhead is the fitting at top of service mast/raceway where overhead service conductors exit to prevent water entering. NEC 230.53 requires service-head (weatherhead) to be located above service-drop conductors and arranged to prevent water from entering raceway. Also 230.54? Actually 230.53 Service Heads and Goosenecks. Service heads must have conductors arranged to drain. Unsealed penetration could allow water into raceway/service equipment, leading to corrosion, short circuits, electrical faults, potential arc flash, shock. Severity maybe high due to service entrance energized? Work in-progress maybe not yet energized? But service entrance & metering could be near energization. Immediate controls: stop work, do not energize, seal penetration per manufacturer/code, inspect/replace damaged components, verify weatherhead installed with proper drip loop/sealant, schedule re-inspection.

Need fill JSON fields. decision: fail. risk: high? Could be medium if not energized. But service entrance unsealed weatherhead penetration is a code violation and water intrusion hazard; severity high if energized. Use high. work_stage: in-progress. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete: maybe 60? Service entrance & metering in progress. value 0. sound_source etc default empty string/0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0. rate 0. order empty. workable empty. findings: list. actions: list. rationale. references: NEC Art. 230.53, 230.54? Let's check exact articles.

NEC Article 230 Services. Part IV. Service-Entrance Conductors. 230.42 Size and Rating. 230.43 Wiring Methods. 230.44 Cable Trays. 230.46 Spliced Conductors. 230.49 Protection Against Accidental Contact. 230.50 Protection from Damage. 230.51 Raceways to Drain. 230.52 Individual Closed Raceways. 230.53 Service Heads and Goosenecks. 230.54 Wiring Methods and Materials. 230.56 Service Entrance Cable. 230.57 Securing and Supporting. 230.58 Induced Currents. 230.60 Underground Service. 230.62 Service Equipment — General. 230.66 Marking. 230.70 General. 230.71 Maximum Number of Disconnects. etc.

Weatherhead penetration unsealed relates to 230.53 Service Heads and Goosenecks, and 230.51 Raceways to Drain. Also 110.3(B) installation per listing. Use references: "NEC Art. 230.53", "NEC Art. 230.51", "NEC 110.3(B)". Maybe "NEC Art. 230.54" not needed.

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside. We must output exactly one valid JSON object. The user asked write to /app/answer.json but as assistant we output JSON content. Since no file system, we return JSON. The instruction says do not include prose, Markdown, code fences, or commentary outside JSON. So final should be just JSON. Need include all fields. Some fields not applicable: sound_source maybe "" or null? Schema says string. Use "". event_time 0.0, rate 0.0, order [], workable [], value 0. percent_complete estimate. Let's set 55. confidence 0.9.

Findings: "Unsealed weatherhead penetration allows water intrusion into service raceway/equipment." "Condition violates NEC requirements for weatherhead installation and water exclusion." "Potential for conductor insulation degradation, corrosion, short circuit, arc flash, and shock if energized." Actions: "Stop work and do not energize service until corrected." "Remove/replace or properly seal weatherhead penetration per manufacturer listing and NEC." "Ensure service head is above conductors, raceway drains, and drip loop is provided." "Inspect interior raceway/equipment for moisture or damage; dry or replace as needed." "Re-inspect and obtain third-party inspection before energization." Rationale: "A…
